query: History of the artificialintelligenceact.eu national implementation plans tracker: how many EU member states classified as Clear (designated both market surveillance and notifying authorities) over time in late 2025 and 2026. What is the trend/rate of member states moving to Clear status?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key takeaway:

## Answer

The artificialintelligenceact.eu tracker classifies EU member states as **Clear** (both market surveillance and notifying authorities designated), **Partial clarity**, or **Unclear**.

**The most important finding is a documentation nuance:** the tracker's headline "Clear" count did **not** climb gradually through late 2025/2026. Internet Archive snapshots show the summary was frozen at **"3 Clear / 10 Partial / 14 Unclear" (last update 19 May 2025) continuously from August 2025 through 16 June 2026**, then jumped in a single revision dated **17 June 2026 to 9 Clear / 12 Partial / 6 Unclear**.

**Trajectory:**
- Net movement: **+6 Clear states (3 → 9)** over ~13 months, but batched into one update — so the tracker does **not** support a smooth month-by-month rate. Best characterized as a **step change of +6 recorded on 17 June 2026** (~0.5 states/month if smoothed).
- Direction is monotonically upward: Clear 3→9, Unclear 14→6, Partial ~flat (10→12); no state regressed.
- Current 9 "Clear": Cyprus, Denmark, Finland, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Lithuania, Slovenia, Spain.

**Corroboration & caveats:** Independent trackers (aiacto: "8 of 27 ready"; MIAI/Technology's Legal Edge) confirm slow, lagging real-world designation against the 2 August 2025 deadline. The tracker's headline lags reality and should be cross-checked against the European Commission's consolidated authorities list; I could not extract reliable intermediate Clear counts because the site's summary text was static during the intervening window.

key_facts: The artificialintelligenceact.eu tracker classifies EU member states as 'Clear' (both market surveillance and notifying authorities designated), 'Partial clarity', or 'Unclear' under Article 70 of the AI Act; the designation deadline was 2 August 2025.
The tracker's summary paragraph was frozen at 'Last update 19 May 2025 / 3 Clear / 10 Partial / 14 Unclear' continuously across all archived snapshots from 2 Aug 2025 through 16 June 2026.

The 'Clear' count jumped from 3 to 9 in a single page revision dated 17 June 2026 (visible in the 3 July 2026 snapshot and the live 12 July 2026 page) — a batched documentation update, not a gradual monthly climb.
As of 17 June 2026 the tracker reports 9 Clear, 12 Partial clarity, and 6 Unclear member states.
The 9 current 'Clear' states are Cyprus, Denmark, Finland, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Lithuania, Slovenia, Spain; the 6 'Unclear' are Austria, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Greece, Latvia.
The early-2025 'Clear' trio was Denmark, Hungary, and Malta/Luxembourg (names drifted between snapshots though the count stayed at 3).
Net change over the observed window is +6 Clear states (3 to 9) over ~13 months, but batched into one update, so no reliable intra-period rate can be derived from this source.
The trend direction is monotonically upward: Clear 3->9, Unclear 14->6, Partial 10->12; no member state moved backward out of Clear.

Independent sources corroborate slow real-world progress: an aiacto analysis (early 2026) cited '8 of 27 member states ready', and legal/academic commentary said a majority of states were behind schedule at the August 2025 deadline.
The Digital Omnibus provisional agreement (7 May 2026) deferred high-risk Annex III obligations to December 2027, reducing time pressure on some designations.

data_points.metric: Member states classified 'Clear' (tracker, archived 2026-07-03, last update 17 June 2026)
Member states classified 'Partial clarity' (17 June 2026 update)
Net increase in 'Clear' member states over observed window (May 2025 to June 2026)
Member states 'ready' per independent aiacto analysis (early 2026)
